mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving near-term equity outperformance over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and acceleration in core inflation signals supply chain bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of observed episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rising real disposable income fuels consumption-driven earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate profits catch up to demand.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when RSAFS trends upward for three consecutive months, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader economic momentum, directly translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as construction and related sectors drive index weightings.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of rolling 12-month windows, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.50%, the market prices in a tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 0.50%, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% in a walk-forward test spanning multiple rate-hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating non-farm payrolls growth and rising real savings indicates a transition from fiscal stimulus to organic economic strength, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings quality improves.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth exceeding 4.0% annualized, demand outpaces supply capacity, causing inflationary pressures that trigger rate-hike fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 4.0%, or the negative predictive power disappears when CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0% year-over-year.
